arts.ttl: New Kick It Out CEO Okafor: There's a lack of accountability within football
arts.sum: Kick It Out's new CEO Samuel Okafor says there is a lack of accountability in football when it comes to under-represented communities.

arts.ttl: Pulisic and AC Milan have no room for error in the UEFA Champions League
arts.sum: Currently, Fonseca’s side is part of the group of teams that have yet to earn a point in the new UEFA Champions League, alongside other notable squads like RB Leipzig, Girona, and RB Salzburg. After two matches in the group stage, they have yet to earn any points, and this Tuesday, they will face Club Brugge at San Siro for the third matchday.

arts.ttl: AC Milan v Club Brugge Preview | Belgian side hoping to inflict third Champions League defeat on their opponents
arts.sum: Club Brugge travel to Milan on Tuesday night hoping to give the home side a third defeat in this seasons Champions League. AC have lost both their opening games, one…

On the eve of AC Milan’s Champions League clash with Club Brugge, Dutch midfielder Tijjani Reijnders addressed the media alongside manager Paulo Fonseca. The summer signing shared his thoughts on his role, ambitions, and recent red card incident. When asked about how Champions League matches could aid his development, Reijnders emphasized the importance of being decisive: “It’s important for me to be decisive. We midfielders are the engine of the team. We did well against Udinese, tomorrow it will be important to play in the same way.” Addressing the difference in refereeing standards between domestic and European competitions, particularly in The post Milan’s Reijnders: “I’m a ‘box-to-box’ No.8” appeared first on FootItalia.com.
